2605.00572.title: Instance-Aware Parameter Configuration in Bilevel Late Acceptance Hill Climbing for the Electric Capacitated Vehicle Routing Problem
2605.00572.paper_summary: Algorithm performance in combinatorial optimization is highly sensitive to parameter settings, while a single globally tuned configuration often fails to exploit the heterogeneity of instances. This limitation is particularly evident in the Electric Capacitated Vehicle Routing Problem, where instances differ in structure, demand patterns, and energy constraints. This paper investigates instance-aware parameter configuration for Bilevel Late Acceptance Hill Climbing, a state-of-the-art metaheuristic for the Electric Capacitated Vehicle Routing Problem. An offline tuning procedure is used to obtain instance-specific parameter labels, which are then mapped from instance features via a regression model to enable parameter prediction for unseen instances prior to execution. Experimental results on the IEEE WCCI 2020 benchmark and its extensions show that the proposed approach achieves an average objective value reduction of $0.28\%$ across eight held-out test instances relative to a globally tuned configuration. This corresponds to a significant cost reduction in multimillion-dollar transportation operations.
2605.00572.paper_summary_zh: 算法在組合優化中的性能對參數設置非常敏感，而單一的全局調整配置通常無法充分利用實例的異質性。這一限制在電動容量車輛路由問題中尤為明顯，因為實例在結構、需求模式和能量限制上存在差異。本文研究了針對實例的參數配置，針對雙層延遲接受爬山演算法，這是一種針對電動容量車輛路由問題的最先進元啟發式演算法。使用離線調整程序獲取實例特定的參數標籤，然後通過回歸模型將其從實例特徵映射，以便在執行之前對未見實例進行參數預測。在IEEE WCCI 2020基準及其擴展上的實驗結果顯示，所提出的方法在八個保留測試實例中，相對於全局調整配置實現了平均目標值降低$0.28\%$。這對於數百萬美元的運輸操作來說，對成本的降低具有重要意義。
2605.00572.author: Yinghao Qin et.al.
2605.00572.authors: Yinghao Qin, Xinwei Wang, Mosab Bazargani, Jun Chen
